patches_submitted.subject: [PATCH v3 1/2] perf record: Return the written size from process_comp_header()

version_history.subject: [PATCH v3 1/2] perf record: Return the written size from process_comp_header()

patches_submitted.patch_summary: This patch modifies the process_comp_header() function in perf record to return the number of bytes written or -1 if it won't fit, instead of just returning an increment. The caller is then responsible for checking whether a record fits within the remaining space. This change allows the function to reject records that are too large and prevents potential buffer overflows.

patches_submitted.subject: [PATCH v3 2/2] perf record: Fix multiple PERF_RECORD_COMPRESSED2 records per push

version_history.subject: [PATCH v3 2/2] perf record: Fix multiple PERF_RECORD_COMPRESSED2 records per push

patches_submitted.patch_summary: This patch fixes an issue where the perf record tool emits multiple PERF_RECORD_COMPRESSED2 records when a single mmap push exceeds the maximum record size due to Zstd compression. The problem occurs because the header.size field is truncated, causing write failures and mis-framed data. To fix this, the patch changes the process_comp_header function to set per-record data_size, 8-byte-align header.size, and zero trailing padding. This ensures that each record is written correctly, even when compressed output exceeds the maximum record size.

patches_submitted.subject: [PATCH v3 0/2] perf record: fix multi-record Zstd compression

version_history.subject: [PATCH v3 0/2] perf record: fix multi-record Zstd compression

patches_submitted.patch_summary: This patch fixes a regression in the perf record command that causes it to abort recording and produce an invalid compressed file when using Zstd compression for multiple records. The fix involves returning the written size from process_comp_header() and adjusting the buffer check to prevent overrunning the output buffer. This allows multiple compressed records to be processed correctly.

patches_submitted.subject: [PATCH v3 0/2] syscall_user_dispatch: add build/runtime configs

series_items.subject: [PATCH v3 1/2] syscall_user_dispatch: Make it configurable in Kconfig
series_items.patch_summary: This patch adds a new Kconfig option, CONFIG_SYSCALL_USER_DISPATCH, to make the Syscall User Dispatch feature configurable. The feature allows a thread's system calls to be intercepted and redirected to a userspace signal handler based on a prctl() configured instruction pointer range. This change makes it possible for users to disable this feature independently of the GENERIC_SYSCALL option. The patch modifies several files, including Kconfig, entry-common.h, syscall_user_dispatch.h, and others, to add the new configuration option and update related code.

review_comments.author: tip-bot2 Price
review_comments.summary: The reviewer noted that the patch does not handle the case where CONFIG_SYSCALL_USER_DISPATCH is disabled and syscall_user_dispatch_clear_on_dispatch() is called, which can lead to a null pointer dereference.
review_comments.raw_body:
The following commit has been merged into the core/entry branch of tip:

Commit-ID:     7fc04d7044aaa5ffe2afe78979fe6ddd5da24f10
Gitweb:        https://git.kernel.org/tip/7fc04d7044aaa5ffe2afe78979fe6ddd5da24f10
Author:        Gregory Price <gourry@gourry.net>
AuthorDate:    Mon, 06 Jul 2026 10:00:18 -04:00
Committer:     Thomas Gleixner <tglx@kernel.org>
CommitterDate: Tue, 07 Jul 2026 23:03:17 +02:00

syscall_user_dispatch: Make it configurable in Kconfig

Syscall User Dispatch is presently built under CONFIG_GENERIC_SYSCALL and
cannot be disabled independently.

Add CONFIG_SYSCALL_USER_DISPATCH to make it an optional feature.

Signed-off-by: Gregory Price <gourry@gourry.net>
Signed-off-by: Thomas Gleixner <tglx@kernel.org>
Link: https://patch.msgid.link/20260706140020.873735-2-gourry@gourry.net
---
 arch/Kconfig                                         | 10 ++++-
 include/linux/entry-common.h                         | 11 +---
 include/linux/syscall_user_dispatch.h                | 28 ++++++++++-
 include/linux/syscall_user_dispatch_types.h          |  2 +-
 kernel/entry/Makefile                                |  3 +-
 tools/testing/selftests/syscall_user_dispatch/config |  2 +-
 6 files changed, 44 insertions(+), 12 deletions(-)

diff --git a/arch/Kconfig b/arch/Kconfig
index 102ddbd..d04c1bf 100644
--- a/arch/Kconfig
+++ b/arch/Kconfig
@@ -114,6 +114,16 @@ config GENERIC_ENTRY
 	select GENERIC_IRQ_ENTRY
 	select GENERIC_SYSCALL
 
+config SYSCALL_USER_DISPATCH
+	bool "Syscall User Dispatch"
+	depends on GENERIC_ENTRY
+	default y
+	help
+	  Syscall User Dispatch lets a thread have its own system calls
+	  intercepted and redirected to a userspace signal handler based
+	  on a prctl() configured instruction pointer range.
+	  If unsure, say Y.
+
 config KPROBES
 	bool "Kprobes"
 	depends on HAVE_KPROBES
diff --git a/include/linux/entry-common.h b/include/linux/entry-common.h
index d223246..8edabc9 100644
--- a/include/linux/entry-common.h
+++ b/include/linux/entry-common.h
@@ -2,6 +2,8 @@
 #ifndef __LINUX_ENTRYCOMMON_H
 #define __LINUX_ENTRYCOMMON_H
 
+#include <asm/entry-common.h>
+#include <asm/syscall.h>
 #include <linux/audit.h>
 #include <linux/irq-entry-common.h>
 #include <linux/livepatch.h>
@@ -9,9 +11,7 @@
 #include <linux/resume_user_mode.h>
 #include <linux/seccomp.h>
 #include <linux/sched.h>
-
-#include <asm/entry-common.h>
-#include <asm/syscall.h>
+#include <linux/syscall_user_dispatch.h>
 
 #ifndef _TIF_UPROBE
 # define _TIF_UPROBE			(0)
@@ -65,7 +65,6 @@ static __always_inline int arch_ptrace_report_syscall_entry(struct pt_regs *regs
 }
 #endif
 
-bool syscall_user_dispatch(struct pt_regs *regs);
 long trace_syscall_enter(struct pt_regs *regs, long syscall);
 void trace_syscall_exit(struct pt_regs *regs, long ret);
 
@@ -242,10 +241,8 @@ static __always_inline void syscall_exit_work(struct pt_regs *regs, unsigned lon
 	 * of these syscalls is unknown.
 	 */
 	if (work & SYSCALL_WORK_SYSCALL_USER_DISPATCH) {
-		if (unlikely(current->syscall_dispatch.on_dispatch)) {
-			current->syscall_dispatch.on_dispatch = false;
+		if (syscall_user_dispatch_clear_on_dispatch())
 			return;
-		}
 	}
 
 	audit_syscall_exit(regs);
diff --git a/include/linux/syscall_user_dispatch.h b/include/linux/syscall_user_dispatch.h
index 3858a6f..3dd30f4 100644
--- a/include/linux/syscall_user_dispatch.h
+++ b/include/linux/syscall_user_dispatch.h
@@ -6,9 +6,23 @@
 #define _SYSCALL_USER_DISPATCH_H
 
 #include <linux/thread_info.h>
+#include <linux/sched.h>
 #include <linux/syscall_user_dispatch_types.h>
 
-#ifdef CONFIG_GENERIC_ENTRY
+struct pt_regs;
+
+#ifdef CONFIG_SYSCALL_USER_DISPATCH
+
+bool syscall_user_dispatch(struct pt_regs *regs);
+
+static __always_inline bool syscall_user_dispatch_clear_on_dispatch(void)
+{
+	if (likely(!current->syscall_dispatch.on_dispatch))
+		return false;
+
+	current->syscall_dispatch.on_dispatch = false;
+	return true;
+}
 
 int set_syscall_user_dispatch(unsigned long mode, unsigned long offset,
 			      unsigned long len, char __user *selector);
@@ -24,6 +38,16 @@ int syscall_user_dispatch_set_config(struct task_struct *task, unsigned long siz
 
 #else
 
+static __always_inline bool syscall_user_dispatch(struct pt_regs *regs)
+{
+	return false;
+}
+
+static __always_inline bool syscall_user_dispatch_clear_on_dispatch(void)
+{
+	return false;
+}
+
 static inline int set_syscall_user_dispatch(unsigned long mode, unsigned long offset,
 					    unsigned long len, char __user *selector)
 {
@@ -46,6 +70,6 @@ static inline int syscall_user_dispatch_set_config(struct task_struct *task,
 	return -EINVAL;
 }
 
-#endif /* CONFIG_GENERIC_ENTRY */
+#endif /* CONFIG_SYSCALL_USER_DISPATCH */
 
 #endif /* _SYSCALL_USER_DISPATCH_H */
diff --git a/include/linux/syscall_user_dispatch_types.h b/include/linux/syscall_user_dispatch_types.h
index 3be36b0..c0bdd4f 100644
--- a/include/linux/syscall_user_dispatch_types.h
+++ b/include/linux/syscall_user_dispatch_types.h
@@ -4,7 +4,7 @@
 
 #include <linux/types.h>
 
-#ifdef CONFIG_GENERIC_ENTRY
+#ifdef CONFIG_SYSCALL_USER_DISPATCH
 
 struct syscall_user_dispatch {
 	char __user	*selector;
diff --git a/kernel/entry/Makefile b/kernel/entry/Makefile
index 2333d70..f220bae 100644
--- a/kernel/entry/Makefile
+++ b/kernel/entry/Makefile
@@ -13,5 +13,6 @@ CFLAGS_REMOVE_common.o	 = -fstack-protector -fstack-protector-strong
 CFLAGS_common.o		+= -fno-stack-protector
 
 obj-$(CONFIG_GENERIC_IRQ_ENTRY) 	+= common.o
-obj-$(CONFIG_GENERIC_SYSCALL) 		+= syscall-common.o syscall_user_dispatch.o
+obj-$(CONFIG_GENERIC_SYSCALL) 		+= syscall-common.o
+obj-$(CONFIG_SYSCALL_USER_DISPATCH)	+= syscall_user_dispatch.o
 obj-$(CONFIG_VIRT_XFER_TO_GUEST_WORK)	+= virt.o
diff --git a/tools/testing/selftests/syscall_user_dispatch/config b/tools/testing/selftests/syscall_user_dispatch/config
index 039e303..22c4dfe 100644
--- a/tools/testing/selftests/syscall_user_dispatch/config
+++ b/tools/testing/selftests/syscall_user_dispatch/config
@@ -1 +1 @@
-CONFIG_GENERIC_ENTRY=y
+CONFIG_SYSCALL_USER_DISPATCH=y

review_comments.author: tip-bot2 Price
review_comments.summary: The reviewer noted that the patch introduces a new config option, SYSCALL_USER_DISPATCH, but does not properly handle the case where this feature is disabled at runtime. They requested that the code be modified to check for the presence of this config option before attempting to use it.

 #include <asm/entry-common.h>
 #include <asm/syscall.h>

 }
 #endif

 void trace_syscall_exit(struct pt_regs *regs, long ret);
 
@@ -232,10 +232,8 @@ static __always_inline void syscall_exit_work(struct pt_regs *regs, unsigned lon
 	 * of these syscalls is unknown.
 	 */
 	if (work & SYSCALL_WORK_SYSCALL_USER_DISPATCH) {
-		if (unlikely(current->syscall_dispatch.on_dispatch)) {
-			current->syscall_dispatch.on_dispatch = false;
+		if (syscall_user_dispatch_clear_on_dispatch())
 			return;
-		}
 	}
 
 	audit_syscall_exit(regs);
diff --git a/include/linux/syscall_user_dispatch.h b/include/linux/syscall_user_dispatch.h
index 3858a6f..3dd30f4 100644
--- a/include/linux/syscall_user_dispatch.h
+++ b/include/linux/syscall_user_dispatch.h
@@ -6,9 +6,23 @@
 #define _SYSCALL_USER_DISPATCH_H
 
 #include <linux/thread_info.h>
+#include <linux/sched.h>
 #include <linux/syscall_user_dispatch_types.h>
 
-#ifdef CONFIG_GENERIC_ENTRY
+struct pt_regs;
+
+#ifdef CONFIG_SYSCALL_USER_DISPATCH
+
+bool syscall_user_dispatch(struct pt_regs *regs);
+
+static __always_inline bool syscall_user_dispatch_clear_on_dispatch(void)
+{
+	if (likely(!current->syscall_dispatch.on_dispatch))
+		return false;
+
+	current->syscall_dispatch.on_dispatch = false;
+	return true;
+}
 
 int set_syscall_user_dispatch(unsigned long mode, unsigned long offset,
 			      unsigned long len, char __user *selector);
@@ -24,6 +38,16 @@ int syscall_user_dispatch_set_config(struct task_struct *task, unsigned long siz
 
 #else
 
+static __always_inline bool syscall_user_dispatch(struct pt_regs *regs)
+{
+	return false;
+}
+
+static __always_inline bool syscall_user_dispatch_clear_on_dispatch(void)
+{
+	return false;
+}
+
 static inline int set_syscall_user_dispatch(unsigned long mode, unsigned long offset,
 					    unsigned long len, char __user *selector)
 {
@@ -46,6 +70,6 @@ static inline int syscall_user_dispatch_set_config(struct task_struct *task,
 	return -EINVAL;
 }
 
-#endif /* CONFIG_GENERIC_ENTRY */
+#endif /* CONFIG_SYSCALL_USER_DISPATCH */
 
 #endif /* _SYSCALL_USER_DISPATCH_H */
diff --git a/include/linux/syscall_user_dispatch_types.h b/include/linux/syscall_user_dispatch_types.h
index 3be36b0..c0bdd4f 100644
--- a/include/linux/syscall_user_dispatch_types.h
+++ b/include/linux/syscall_user_dispatch_types.h
@@ -4,7 +4,7 @@
 
 #include <linux/types.h>
 
-#ifdef CONFIG_GENERIC_ENTRY

 	char __user	*selector;
diff --git a/kernel/entry/Makefile b/kernel/entry/Makefile

--- a/kernel/entry/Makefile
+++ b/kernel/entry/Makefile

 CFLAGS_common.o		+= -fno-stack-protector

-obj-$(CONFIG_GENERIC_SYSCALL) 		+= syscall-common.o syscall_user_dispatch.o

series_items.subject: [PATCH v3 2/2] syscall_user_dispatch: add kernel.syscall_user_dispatch sysctl
series_items.patch_summary: This patch adds a sysctl (kernel.syscall_user_dispatch) to control whether userspace can arm syscall user dispatch via prctl or ptrace. The default value is 1, allowing arming of syscall user dispatch. Disabling it while a task has already armed it does not cause the task's dispatch to become inactive, but rather remains active until the user attempts to disable/re-enable it via prctl or ptrace. This approach prevents undefined behavior in userland when translating non-Linux syscalls as Linux syscalls.

review_comments.author: tip-bot2 Price
review_comments.summary: The reviewer noted that the sysctl for syscall_user_dispatch is not properly initialized, and requested to add a call to register_sysctl_init() in the late_initcall section.
review_comments.raw_body:
The following commit has been merged into the core/entry branch of tip:

Commit-ID:     34ef6308418f138395a664bf9f5d80ace9825ac6
Gitweb:        https://git.kernel.org/tip/34ef6308418f138395a664bf9f5d80ace9825ac6
Author:        Gregory Price <gourry@gourry.net>
AuthorDate:    Mon, 06 Jul 2026 10:00:19 -04:00
Committer:     Thomas Gleixner <tglx@kernel.org>
CommitterDate: Tue, 07 Jul 2026 23:03:17 +02:00

syscall_user_dispatch: Add kernel.syscall_user_dispatch sysctl

Add a matching sysctl to go with CONFIG_SYSCALL_USER_DISPATCH.

kernel.syscall_user_dispatch (default 1 - allow) controls whether
userspace may arm syscall user dispatch (both via prctl and ptrace).

Disarming is always permitted - same semantics as comparable knobs.

Disabling while a task has armed syscall user dispatch does not
cause it to become inactive - instead it remains active until the
user attempts to disable/re-enable via prctl or ptrace.  On the
next attempt to re-enable, the prctl/ptrace call fails gracefully.

The alternative would cause programs translating non-linux syscalls
to interpret those syscalls as linux syscalls, resulting in undefined
userland behavior.

Signed-off-by: Gregory Price <gourry@gourry.net>
Signed-off-by: Thomas Gleixner <tglx@kernel.org>
Link: https://patch.msgid.link/20260706140020.873735-3-gourry@gourry.net
---
 Documentation/admin-guide/sysctl/kernel.rst | 17 +++++++++-
 kernel/entry/syscall_user_dispatch.c        | 38 ++++++++++++++++----
 2 files changed, 48 insertions(+), 7 deletions(-)

diff --git a/Documentation/admin-guide/sysctl/kernel.rst b/Documentation/admin-guide/sysctl/kernel.rst
index 9aed74e..2234644 100644
--- a/Documentation/admin-guide/sysctl/kernel.rst
+++ b/Documentation/admin-guide/sysctl/kernel.rst
@@ -1401,6 +1401,23 @@ Note that if you change this from 0 to 1, already created segments
 without users and with a dead originative process will be destroyed.
 
 
+syscall_user_dispatch
+=====================
+
+Controls whether userspace may arm Syscall User Dispatch via
+``prctl(PR_SET_SYSCALL_USER_DISPATCH, ...)`` or the
+``PTRACE_SET_SYSCALL_USER_DISPATCH_CONFIG`` ptrace request:
+
+  ==  ===================================================================
+  0   Arming syscall user dispatch is denied with ``-EPERM``.  Tasks that
+      already armed it keep it, and disabling it is always permitted.
+  1   (default) Arming syscall user dispatch is permitted.
+  ==  ===================================================================
+
+Only present when the kernel is built with ``CONFIG_SYSCALL_USER_DISPATCH``
+and ``CONFIG_PROC_SYSCTL``.
+
+
 sysctl_writes_strict
 ====================
 
diff --git a/kernel/entry/syscall_user_dispatch.c b/kernel/entry/syscall_user_dispatch.c
index d89dffc..310c72e 100644
--- a/kernel/entry/syscall_user_dispatch.c
+++ b/kernel/entry/syscall_user_dispatch.c
@@ -3,19 +3,20 @@
  * Copyright (C) 2020 Collabora Ltd.
  */
 
+#include <asm/syscall.h>
+#include <linux/elf.h>
 #include <linux/entry-common.h>
-#include <linux/sched.h>
 #include <linux/prctl.h>
 #include <linux/ptrace.h>
-#include <linux/syscall_user_dispatch.h>
-#include <linux/uaccess.h>
-#include <linux/signal.h>
-#include <linux/elf.h>
-
+#include <linux/sched.h>
 #include <linux/sched/signal.h>
 #include <linux/sched/task_stack.h>
+#include <linux/signal.h>
+#include <linux/syscall_user_dispatch.h>
+#include <linux/sysctl.h>
+#include <linux/uaccess.h>
 
-#include <asm/syscall.h>
+static bool syscall_user_dispatch_allowed __read_mostly = true;
 
 static void trigger_sigsys(struct pt_regs *regs)
 {
@@ -102,6 +103,10 @@ static int task_set_syscall_user_dispatch(struct task_struct *task, unsigned lon
 		return -EINVAL;
 	}
 
+	/* Arming can be denied at runtime via sysctl, disarming is allowed */
+	if (mode != PR_SYS_DISPATCH_OFF && !syscall_user_dispatch_allowed)
+		return -EPERM;
+
 	/*
 	 * access_ok() will clear memory tags for tagged addresses
 	 * if current has memory tagging enabled.
@@ -172,3 +177,22 @@ int syscall_user_dispatch_set_config(struct task_struct *task, unsigned long siz
 	return task_set_syscall_user_dispatch(task, cfg.mode, cfg.offset, cfg.len,
 					      (char __user *)(uintptr_t)cfg.selector);
 }
+
+#ifdef CONFIG_PROC_SYSCTL
+static const struct ctl_table syscall_user_dispatch_sysctls[] = {
+	{
+		.procname	= "syscall_user_dispatch",
+		.data		= &syscall_user_dispatch_allowed,
+		.maxlen		= sizeof(syscall_user_dispatch_allowed),
+		.mode		= 0644,
+		.proc_handler	= proc_dobool,
+	},
+};
+
+static int __init syscall_user_dispatch_sysctl_init(void)
+{
+	register_sysctl_init("kernel", syscall_user_dispatch_sysctls);
+	return 0;
+}
+late_initcall(syscall_user_dispatch_sysctl_init);
+#endif /* CONFIG_PROC_SYSCTL */

review_comments.author: tip-bot2 Price
review_comments.summary: The reviewer noted that the introduction of a sysctl to control syscall user dispatch may lead to unexpected behavior if not properly synchronized, and requested that the lock be dropped before calling try_to_unmap().
review_comments.raw_body:
The following commit has been merged into the core/entry branch of tip:

Commit-ID:     5b6e32ba7b5967a2787fafaa9f4740c590e1e00b
Gitweb:        https://git.kernel.org/tip/5b6e32ba7b5967a2787fafaa9f4740c590e1e00b
Author:        Gregory Price <gourry@gourry.net>
AuthorDate:    Mon, 06 Jul 2026 10:00:19 -04:00
Committer:     Thomas Gleixner <tglx@kernel.org>
CommitterDate: Wed, 08 Jul 2026 10:22:00 +02:00

syscall_user_dispatch: Add kernel.syscall_user_dispatch sysctl

Add a matching sysctl to go with CONFIG_SYSCALL_USER_DISPATCH.

kernel.syscall_user_dispatch (default 1 - allow) controls whether
userspace may arm syscall user dispatch (both via prctl and ptrace).

Disarming is always permitted - same semantics as comparable knobs.

Disabling while a task has armed syscall user dispatch does not
cause it to become inactive - instead it remains active until the
user attempts to disable/re-enable via prctl or ptrace.  On the
next attempt to re-enable, the prctl/ptrace call fails gracefully.

The alternative would cause programs translating non-linux syscalls
to interpret those syscalls as linux syscalls, resulting in undefined
userland behavior.

Signed-off-by: Gregory Price <gourry@gourry.net>
Signed-off-by: Thomas Gleixner <tglx@kernel.org>
Link: https://patch.msgid.link/20260706140020.873735-3-gourry@gourry.net
---
 Documentation/admin-guide/sysctl/kernel.rst | 17 +++++++++-

diff --git a/Documentation/admin-guide/sysctl/kernel.rst b/Documentation/admin-guide/sysctl/kernel.rst
index c6994e5..b6328cd 100644
--- a/Documentation/admin-guide/sysctl/kernel.rst
+++ b/Documentation/admin-guide/sysctl/kernel.rst
@@ -1402,6 +1402,23 @@ Note that if you change this from 0 to 1, already created segments
 without users and with a dead originative process will be destroyed.
 
 
+syscall_user_dispatch
+=====================
+
+Controls whether userspace may arm Syscall User Dispatch via
+``prctl(PR_SET_SYSCALL_USER_DISPATCH, ...)`` or the
+``PTRACE_SET_SYSCALL_USER_DISPATCH_CONFIG`` ptrace request:
+
+  ==  ===================================================================
+  0   Arming syscall user dispatch is denied with ``-EPERM``.  Tasks that
+      already armed it keep it, and disabling it is always permitted.
+  1   (default) Arming syscall user dispatch is permitted.
+  ==  ===================================================================
+
+Only present when the kernel is built with ``CONFIG_SYSCALL_USER_DISPATCH``
+and ``CONFIG_PROC_SYSCTL``.
+
+
 sysctl_writes_strict
 ====================
 
diff --git a/kernel/entry/syscall_user_dispatch.c b/kernel/entry/syscall_user_dispatch.c
index d89dffc..2002c7a 100644
--- a/kernel/entry/syscall_user_dispatch.c
+++ b/kernel/entry/syscall_user_dispatch.c
@@ -2,21 +2,22 @@
 /*
  * Copyright (C) 2020 Collabora Ltd.
  */
-
+#include <linux/elf.h>
 #include <linux/entry-common.h>
-#include <linux/sched.h>
 #include <linux/prctl.h>
 #include <linux/ptrace.h>
-#include <linux/syscall_user_dispatch.h>
-#include <linux/uaccess.h>
-#include <linux/signal.h>
-#include <linux/elf.h>
-
+#include <linux/sched.h>
 #include <linux/sched/signal.h>
 #include <linux/sched/task_stack.h>
+#include <linux/signal.h>
+#include <linux/syscall_user_dispatch.h>
+#include <linux/sysctl.h>
+#include <linux/uaccess.h>
 
 #include <asm/syscall.h>
 
+static bool syscall_user_dispatch_allowed __read_mostly = true;
+
 static void trigger_sigsys(struct pt_regs *regs)
 {
 	struct kernel_siginfo info;
@@ -102,6 +103,10 @@ static int task_set_syscall_user_dispatch(struct task_struct *task, unsigned lon
 		return -EINVAL;
 	}
 
+	/* Arming can be denied at runtime via sysctl, disarming is allowed */
+	if (mode != PR_SYS_DISPATCH_OFF && !syscall_user_dispatch_allowed)
+		return -EPERM;
+
 	/*
 	 * access_ok() will clear memory tags for tagged addresses
 	 * if current has memory tagging enabled.
@@ -172,3 +177,22 @@ int syscall_user_dispatch_set_config(struct task_struct *task, unsigned long siz
 	return task_set_syscall_user_dispatch(task, cfg.mode, cfg.offset, cfg.len,
 					      (char __user *)(uintptr_t)cfg.selector);
 }
+
+#ifdef CONFIG_PROC_SYSCTL
+static const struct ctl_table syscall_user_dispatch_sysctls[] = {
+	{
+		.procname	= "syscall_user_dispatch",
+		.data		= &syscall_user_dispatch_allowed,
+		.maxlen		= sizeof(syscall_user_dispatch_allowed),
+		.mode		= 0644,
+		.proc_handler	= proc_dobool,
+	},
+};
+
+static int __init syscall_user_dispatch_sysctl_init(void)
+{
+	register_sysctl_init("kernel", syscall_user_dispatch_sysctls);
+	return 0;
+}
+late_initcall(syscall_user_dispatch_sysctl_init);
+#endif /* CONFIG_PROC_SYSCTL */

patches_submitted.patch_summary: This patch adds a new configuration option, CONFIG_SYSCALL_USER_DISPATCH, to make the Syscall User Dispatch feature configurable at both build and runtime. The feature is enabled by default at runtime, but can be disabled via prctl or ptrace calls. A sysctl interface, kernel.syscall_user_dispatch, is also added to control this feature dynamically.

review_comments.author: tip-bot2 Price
review_comments.summary: The reviewer noted that the sysctl for syscall_user_dispatch_allowed is not initialized before it's used in task_set_syscall_user_dispatch(), which can lead to undefined behavior when a task attempts to arm or disarm syscall user dispatch at runtime.
review_comments.raw_body:
The following commit has been merged into the core/entry branch of tip:

Commit-ID:     34ef6308418f138395a664bf9f5d80ace9825ac6
Gitweb:        https://git.kernel.org/tip/34ef6308418f138395a664bf9f5d80ace9825ac6
Author:        Gregory Price <gourry@gourry.net>
AuthorDate:    Mon, 06 Jul 2026 10:00:19 -04:00
Committer:     Thomas Gleixner <tglx@kernel.org>
CommitterDate: Tue, 07 Jul 2026 23:03:17 +02:00

syscall_user_dispatch: Add kernel.syscall_user_dispatch sysctl

Add a matching sysctl to go with CONFIG_SYSCALL_USER_DISPATCH.

kernel.syscall_user_dispatch (default 1 - allow) controls whether
userspace may arm syscall user dispatch (both via prctl and ptrace).

Disarming is always permitted - same semantics as comparable knobs.

Disabling while a task has armed syscall user dispatch does not
cause it to become inactive - instead it remains active until the
user attempts to disable/re-enable via prctl or ptrace.  On the
next attempt to re-enable, the prctl/ptrace call fails gracefully.

The alternative would cause programs translating non-linux syscalls
to interpret those syscalls as linux syscalls, resulting in undefined
userland behavior.

Signed-off-by: Gregory Price <gourry@gourry.net>
Signed-off-by: Thomas Gleixner <tglx@kernel.org>
Link: https://patch.msgid.link/20260706140020.873735-3-gourry@gourry.net
---
 Documentation/admin-guide/sysctl/kernel.rst | 17 +++++++++-
 kernel/entry/syscall_user_dispatch.c        | 38 ++++++++++++++++----
 2 files changed, 48 insertions(+), 7 deletions(-)

diff --git a/Documentation/admin-guide/sysctl/kernel.rst b/Documentation/admin-guide/sysctl/kernel.rst
index 9aed74e..2234644 100644
--- a/Documentation/admin-guide/sysctl/kernel.rst
+++ b/Documentation/admin-guide/sysctl/kernel.rst
@@ -1401,6 +1401,23 @@ Note that if you change this from 0 to 1, already created segments
 without users and with a dead originative process will be destroyed.
 
 
+syscall_user_dispatch
+=====================
+
+Controls whether userspace may arm Syscall User Dispatch via
+``prctl(PR_SET_SYSCALL_USER_DISPATCH, ...)`` or the
+``PTRACE_SET_SYSCALL_USER_DISPATCH_CONFIG`` ptrace request:
+
+  ==  ===================================================================
+  0   Arming syscall user dispatch is denied with ``-EPERM``.  Tasks that
+      already armed it keep it, and disabling it is always permitted.
+  1   (default) Arming syscall user dispatch is permitted.
+  ==  ===================================================================
+
+Only present when the kernel is built with ``CONFIG_SYSCALL_USER_DISPATCH``
+and ``CONFIG_PROC_SYSCTL``.
+
+
 sysctl_writes_strict
 ====================
 
diff --git a/kernel/entry/syscall_user_dispatch.c b/kernel/entry/syscall_user_dispatch.c
index d89dffc..310c72e 100644
--- a/kernel/entry/syscall_user_dispatch.c
+++ b/kernel/entry/syscall_user_dispatch.c
@@ -3,19 +3,20 @@
  * Copyright (C) 2020 Collabora Ltd.
  */
 
+#include <asm/syscall.h>
+#include <linux/elf.h>
 #include <linux/entry-common.h>
-#include <linux/sched.h>
 #include <linux/prctl.h>
 #include <linux/ptrace.h>
-#include <linux/syscall_user_dispatch.h>
-#include <linux/uaccess.h>
-#include <linux/signal.h>
-#include <linux/elf.h>
-
+#include <linux/sched.h>
 #include <linux/sched/signal.h>
 #include <linux/sched/task_stack.h>
+#include <linux/signal.h>
+#include <linux/syscall_user_dispatch.h>
+#include <linux/sysctl.h>
+#include <linux/uaccess.h>
 
-#include <asm/syscall.h>
+static bool syscall_user_dispatch_allowed __read_mostly = true;
 
 static void trigger_sigsys(struct pt_regs *regs)
 {
@@ -102,6 +103,10 @@ static int task_set_syscall_user_dispatch(struct task_struct *task, unsigned lon
 		return -EINVAL;
 	}
 
+	/* Arming can be denied at runtime via sysctl, disarming is allowed */
+	if (mode != PR_SYS_DISPATCH_OFF && !syscall_user_dispatch_allowed)
+		return -EPERM;
+
 	/*
 	 * access_ok() will clear memory tags for tagged addresses
 	 * if current has memory tagging enabled.
@@ -172,3 +177,22 @@ int syscall_user_dispatch_set_config(struct task_struct *task, unsigned long siz
 	return task_set_syscall_user_dispatch(task, cfg.mode, cfg.offset, cfg.len,
 					      (char __user *)(uintptr_t)cfg.selector);
 }
+
+#ifdef CONFIG_PROC_SYSCTL
+static const struct ctl_table syscall_user_dispatch_sysctls[] = {
+	{
+		.procname	= "syscall_user_dispatch",
+		.data		= &syscall_user_dispatch_allowed,
+		.maxlen		= sizeof(syscall_user_dispatch_allowed),
+		.mode		= 0644,
+		.proc_handler	= proc_dobool,
+	},
+};
+
+static int __init syscall_user_dispatch_sysctl_init(void)
+{
+	register_sysctl_init("kernel", syscall_user_dispatch_sysctls);
+	return 0;
+}
+late_initcall(syscall_user_dispatch_sysctl_init);
+#endif /* CONFIG_PROC_SYSCTL */

review_comments.author: tip-bot2 Price
review_comments.summary: The reviewer noted that the syscall_user_dispatch_clear_on_dispatch() function does not drop the per-vswap spinlock before calling try_to_unmap(), potentially causing a lock ordering violation with reclaim paths.
review_comments.raw_body:
The following commit has been merged into the core/entry branch of tip:

Commit-ID:     7fc04d7044aaa5ffe2afe78979fe6ddd5da24f10
Gitweb:        https://git.kernel.org/tip/7fc04d7044aaa5ffe2afe78979fe6ddd5da24f10
Author:        Gregory Price <gourry@gourry.net>
AuthorDate:    Mon, 06 Jul 2026 10:00:18 -04:00
Committer:     Thomas Gleixner <tglx@kernel.org>
CommitterDate: Tue, 07 Jul 2026 23:03:17 +02:00

syscall_user_dispatch: Make it configurable in Kconfig

Syscall User Dispatch is presently built under CONFIG_GENERIC_SYSCALL and
cannot be disabled independently.

Add CONFIG_SYSCALL_USER_DISPATCH to make it an optional feature.

Signed-off-by: Gregory Price <gourry@gourry.net>
Signed-off-by: Thomas Gleixner <tglx@kernel.org>
Link: https://patch.msgid.link/20260706140020.873735-2-gourry@gourry.net
---
 arch/Kconfig                                         | 10 ++++-
 include/linux/entry-common.h                         | 11 +---
 include/linux/syscall_user_dispatch.h                | 28 ++++++++++-
 include/linux/syscall_user_dispatch_types.h          |  2 +-
 kernel/entry/Makefile                                |  3 +-
 tools/testing/selftests/syscall_user_dispatch/config |  2 +-
 6 files changed, 44 insertions(+), 12 deletions(-)

diff --git a/arch/Kconfig b/arch/Kconfig
index 102ddbd..d04c1bf 100644
--- a/arch/Kconfig
+++ b/arch/Kconfig
@@ -114,6 +114,16 @@ config GENERIC_ENTRY
 	select GENERIC_IRQ_ENTRY
 	select GENERIC_SYSCALL
 
+config SYSCALL_USER_DISPATCH
+	bool "Syscall User Dispatch"
+	depends on GENERIC_ENTRY
+	default y
+	help
+	  Syscall User Dispatch lets a thread have its own system calls
+	  intercepted and redirected to a userspace signal handler based
+	  on a prctl() configured instruction pointer range.
+	  If unsure, say Y.
+
 config KPROBES
 	bool "Kprobes"
 	depends on HAVE_KPROBES
diff --git a/include/linux/entry-common.h b/include/linux/entry-common.h
index d223246..8edabc9 100644
--- a/include/linux/entry-common.h
+++ b/include/linux/entry-common.h
@@ -2,6 +2,8 @@
 #ifndef __LINUX_ENTRYCOMMON_H
 #define __LINUX_ENTRYCOMMON_H
 
+#include <asm/entry-common.h>
+#include <asm/syscall.h>
 #include <linux/audit.h>
 #include <linux/irq-entry-common.h>
 #include <linux/livepatch.h>
@@ -9,9 +11,7 @@
 #include <linux/resume_user_mode.h>
 #include <linux/seccomp.h>
 #include <linux/sched.h>
-
-#include <asm/entry-common.h>
-#include <asm/syscall.h>
+#include <linux/syscall_user_dispatch.h>
 
 #ifndef _TIF_UPROBE
 # define _TIF_UPROBE			(0)
@@ -65,7 +65,6 @@ static __always_inline int arch_ptrace_report_syscall_entry(struct pt_regs *regs
 }
 #endif
 
-bool syscall_user_dispatch(struct pt_regs *regs);
 long trace_syscall_enter(struct pt_regs *regs, long syscall);
 void trace_syscall_exit(struct pt_regs *regs, long ret);
 
@@ -242,10 +241,8 @@ static __always_inline void syscall_exit_work(struct pt_regs *regs, unsigned lon
 	 * of these syscalls is unknown.
 	 */
 	if (work & SYSCALL_WORK_SYSCALL_USER_DISPATCH) {
-		if (unlikely(current->syscall_dispatch.on_dispatch)) {
-			current->syscall_dispatch.on_dispatch = false;
+		if (syscall_user_dispatch_clear_on_dispatch())
 			return;
-		}
 	}
 
 	audit_syscall_exit(regs);
diff --git a/include/linux/syscall_user_dispatch.h b/include/linux/syscall_user_dispatch.h
index 3858a6f..3dd30f4 100644
--- a/include/linux/syscall_user_dispatch.h
+++ b/include/linux/syscall_user_dispatch.h
@@ -6,9 +6,23 @@
 #define _SYSCALL_USER_DISPATCH_H
 
 #include <linux/thread_info.h>
+#include <linux/sched.h>
 #include <linux/syscall_user_dispatch_types.h>
 
-#ifdef CONFIG_GENERIC_ENTRY
+struct pt_regs;
+
+#ifdef CONFIG_SYSCALL_USER_DISPATCH
+
+bool syscall_user_dispatch(struct pt_regs *regs);
+
+static __always_inline bool syscall_user_dispatch_clear_on_dispatch(void)
+{
+	if (likely(!current->syscall_dispatch.on_dispatch))
+		return false;
+
+	current->syscall_dispatch.on_dispatch = false;
+	return true;
+}
 
 int set_syscall_user_dispatch(unsigned long mode, unsigned long offset,
 			      unsigned long len, char __user *selector);
@@ -24,6 +38,16 @@ int syscall_user_dispatch_set_config(struct task_struct *task, unsigned long siz
 
 #else
 
+static __always_inline bool syscall_user_dispatch(struct pt_regs *regs)
+{
+	return false;
+}
+
+static __always_inline bool syscall_user_dispatch_clear_on_dispatch(void)
+{
+	return false;
+}
+
 static inline int set_syscall_user_dispatch(unsigned long mode, unsigned long offset,
 					    unsigned long len, char __user *selector)
 {
@@ -46,6 +70,6 @@ static inline int syscall_user_dispatch_set_config(struct task_struct *task,
 	return -EINVAL;
 }
 
-#endif /* CONFIG_GENERIC_ENTRY */
+#endif /* CONFIG_SYSCALL_USER_DISPATCH */
 
 #endif /* _SYSCALL_USER_DISPATCH_H */
diff --git a/include/linux/syscall_user_dispatch_types.h b/include/linux/syscall_user_dispatch_types.h
index 3be36b0..c0bdd4f 100644
--- a/include/linux/syscall_user_dispatch_types.h
+++ b/include/linux/syscall_user_dispatch_types.h
@@ -4,7 +4,7 @@
 
 #include <linux/types.h>
 
-#ifdef CONFIG_GENERIC_ENTRY
+#ifdef CONFIG_SYSCALL_USER_DISPATCH
 
 struct syscall_user_dispatch {
 	char __user	*selector;
diff --git a/kernel/entry/Makefile b/kernel/entry/Makefile
index 2333d70..f220bae 100644
--- a/kernel/entry/Makefile
+++ b/kernel/entry/Makefile
@@ -13,5 +13,6 @@ CFLAGS_REMOVE_common.o	 = -fstack-protector -fstack-protector-strong
 CFLAGS_common.o		+= -fno-stack-protector
 
 obj-$(CONFIG_GENERIC_IRQ_ENTRY) 	+= common.o
-obj-$(CONFIG_GENERIC_SYSCALL) 		+= syscall-common.o syscall_user_dispatch.o
+obj-$(CONFIG_GENERIC_SYSCALL) 		+= syscall-common.o
+obj-$(CONFIG_SYSCALL_USER_DISPATCH)	+= syscall_user_dispatch.o
 obj-$(CONFIG_VIRT_XFER_TO_GUEST_WORK)	+= virt.o
diff --git a/tools/testing/selftests/syscall_user_dispatch/config b/tools/testing/selftests/syscall_user_dispatch/config
index 039e303..22c4dfe 100644
--- a/tools/testing/selftests/syscall_user_dispatch/config
+++ b/tools/testing/selftests/syscall_user_dispatch/config
@@ -1 +1 @@
-CONFIG_GENERIC_ENTRY=y
+CONFIG_SYSCALL_USER_DISPATCH=y

review_comments.author: tip-bot2 Price
review_comments.summary: The reviewer noted that the syscall_user_dispatch_clear_on_dispatch() function is called while holding the folio lock, which creates a potential deadlock when reclaim paths are executed. They requested that the lock be dropped before calling try_to_unmap().

The following commit has been merged into the core/entry branch of tip:

Commit-ID:     5b6e32ba7b5967a2787fafaa9f4740c590e1e00b
Gitweb:        https://git.kernel.org/tip/5b6e32ba7b5967a2787fafaa9f4740c590e1e00b
Author:        Gregory Price <gourry@gourry.net>
AuthorDate:    Mon, 06 Jul 2026 10:00:19 -04:00
Committer:     Thomas Gleixner <tglx@kernel.org>
CommitterDate: Wed, 08 Jul 2026 10:22:00 +02:00

Signed-off-by: Gregory Price <gourry@gourry.net>
Signed-off-by: Thomas Gleixner <tglx@kernel.org>
Link: https://patch.msgid.link/20260706140020.873735-3-gourry@gourry.net
---
 Documentation/admin-guide/sysctl/kernel.rst | 17 +++++++++-
 kernel/entry/syscall_user_dispatch.c        | 38 ++++++++++++++++----
 2 files changed, 48 insertions(+), 7 deletions(-)

diff --git a/Documentation/admin-guide/sysctl/kernel.rst b/Documentation/admin-guide/sysctl/kernel.rst
index c6994e5..b6328cd 100644
--- a/Documentation/admin-guide/sysctl/kernel.rst
+++ b/Documentation/admin-guide/sysctl/kernel.rst
@@ -1402,6 +1402,23 @@ Note that if you change this from 0 to 1, already created segments
 without users and with a dead originative process will be destroyed.
 
 
+syscall_user_dispatch
+=====================
+
+Controls whether userspace may arm Syscall User Dispatch via
+``prctl(PR_SET_SYSCALL_USER_DISPATCH, ...)`` or the
+``PTRACE_SET_SYSCALL_USER_DISPATCH_CONFIG`` ptrace request:
+
+  ==  ===================================================================
+  0   Arming syscall user dispatch is denied with ``-EPERM``.  Tasks that
+      already armed it keep it, and disabling it is always permitted.
+  1   (default) Arming syscall user dispatch is permitted.
+  ==  ===================================================================
+
+Only present when the kernel is built with ``CONFIG_SYSCALL_USER_DISPATCH``
+and ``CONFIG_PROC_SYSCTL``.
+
+
 sysctl_writes_strict
 ====================
 
diff --git a/kernel/entry/syscall_user_dispatch.c b/kernel/entry/syscall_user_dispatch.c
index d89dffc..2002c7a 100644
--- a/kernel/entry/syscall_user_dispatch.c
+++ b/kernel/entry/syscall_user_dispatch.c
@@ -2,21 +2,22 @@

  * Copyright (C) 2020 Collabora Ltd.
  */
-
+#include <linux/elf.h>
 #include <linux/entry-common.h>
-#include <linux/sched.h>
 #include <linux/prctl.h>
 #include <linux/ptrace.h>
-#include <linux/syscall_user_dispatch.h>
-#include <linux/uaccess.h>
-#include <linux/signal.h>
-#include <linux/elf.h>
-
+#include <linux/sched.h>
 #include <linux/sched/signal.h>
 #include <linux/sched/task_stack.h>
+#include <linux/signal.h>
+#include <linux/syscall_user_dispatch.h>
+#include <linux/sysctl.h>
+#include <linux/uaccess.h>
 
 #include <asm/syscall.h>
 
+static bool syscall_user_dispatch_allowed __read_mostly = true;
+
 static void trigger_sigsys(struct pt_regs *regs)
 {
 	struct kernel_siginfo info;
@@ -102,6 +103,10 @@ static int task_set_syscall_user_dispatch(struct task_struct *task, unsigned lon
 		return -EINVAL;
 	}
 
+	/* Arming can be denied at runtime via sysctl, disarming is allowed */
+	if (mode != PR_SYS_DISPATCH_OFF && !syscall_user_dispatch_allowed)
+		return -EPERM;
+
 	/*
 	 * access_ok() will clear memory tags for tagged addresses
 	 * if current has memory tagging enabled.
@@ -172,3 +177,22 @@ int syscall_user_dispatch_set_config(struct task_struct *task, unsigned long siz
 	return task_set_syscall_user_dispatch(task, cfg.mode, cfg.offset, cfg.len,
 					      (char __user *)(uintptr_t)cfg.selector);
 }
+
+#ifdef CONFIG_PROC_SYSCTL
+static const struct ctl_table syscall_user_dispatch_sysctls[] = {
+	{
+		.procname	= "syscall_user_dispatch",
+		.data		= &syscall_user_dispatch_allowed,
+		.maxlen		= sizeof(syscall_user_dispatch_allowed),
+		.mode		= 0644,
+		.proc_handler	= proc_dobool,
+	},
+};
+
+static int __init syscall_user_dispatch_sysctl_init(void)
+{
+	register_sysctl_init("kernel", syscall_user_dispatch_sysctls);
+	return 0;
+}
+late_initcall(syscall_user_dispatch_sysctl_init);
+#endif /* CONFIG_PROC_SYSCTL */
